audits.name: Control A: closed reversible finite cycle
audits.resource_accounting: none: closed finite permutation, no sink or drawdown
audits.obstruction_status: Killed by T110: a strict scalar monotone cannot live on a finite closed reversible orbit.
audits.verdict: dead: directed cycle blocks a strict scalar monotone

audits.name: Control B: stationary finite Markov chain
audits.obstruction_status: Killed by T122: stationarity forces zero stationary-weighted expected drift, so upward drift is balanced by downward drift.
audits.verdict: dead: directed cycle blocks a strict scalar monotone

audits.name: Test C: finite resource drawdown
audits.resource_accounting: finite stock r=3,2,1,0 with no refill
audits.obstruction_status: Survives only by leaving the T110/T122 assumptions: the model is not closed reversible and not stationary.
audits.equivalence_note: Smallest non-stipulative survivor in this audit: direction is the declared loss of finite future capacity.
audits.verdict: lives only with explicit resource/boundary accounting

audits.name: Test D: maintenance burden with finite repair budget
audits.resource_accounting: maintenance consumes one unit from a finite repair budget per step; health persists only while budget remains
audits.obstruction_status: Survives, but not independently: maintenance creates direction only because a finite repair budget is being depleted.
audits.equivalence_note: Equivalent to resource drawdown plus a persistence task label. Maintenance without finite budget is not shown to produce a new arrow.
audits.verdict: lives only with explicit resource/boundary accounting

audits.name: Test E: finite open boundary with sink fill
transition_rules: S0->S1 p=1 (export one record to sink)
S1->S2 p=1 (export one record to sink)
audits.resource_accounting: system resource is refilled by source; boundary sink capacity fills from 0 to 3
audits.obstruction_status: Survives only when the boundary state is counted. If the sink is not part of the state, this reduces to omitted history.
audits.equivalence_note: Equivalent to resource drawdown in the environment: sink capacity is the resource being consumed.
audits.verdict: lives only with explicit resource/boundary accounting

audits.name: Test F: constructor-restricted transformations
audits.resource_accounting: none internal; reverse transformations are excluded by the admissibility relation
audits.obstruction_status: Formally survives, but direction is imported by stipulating the admissible transformation preorder.
audits.equivalence_note: Smallest formal survivor if stipulation is allowed; weakest physical survivor because it names no substrate cost.
audits.verdict: lives formally, but by stipulated admissibility restriction

smallest_formal_ingredient_set: constructor restriction alone, but only because the reverse transformation is excluded by definition
smallest_nonstipulative_ingredient_set: finite nonrenewed resource drawdown with an absorbing depleted boundary
improved: T128 identifies the first finite models that live after the obstruction stack: strict direction appears only when a resource, sink, boundary, or constructor restriction is explicitly included.
weakened: Maintenance and open-boundary arrows do not emerge as independent ingredients in these fixtures. They reduce to resource drawdown, exported history, sink-capacity accounting, or stipulated constructor admissibility.
claim_impact_recommendation: Preserve H7 only as a resource-accounting or constructor lemma. Do not promote it as a thermodynamic-arrow claim.
open_blocker: The strongest survivor is still an accounting model. A physical upgrade would need a named free-energy or capacity variable and a comparison to standard stochastic thermodynamics.
claim_ledger_update: H7 remains partially supported only in narrowed form. T128 shows that closed reversible and finite stationary Markov controls still fail, while the smallest non-stipulative finite survivor is explicit finite resource drawdown. Maintenance and open boundary survive only as resource/sink/export accounting; constructor restriction survives by stipulation.
suggested_next: Build a thermodynamic calibration for the resource-drawdown survivor and test whether its direction is anything beyond ordinary free-energy or capacity accounting.
